summary: Linked six item translation units by moving them from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py and tightening related item code, headers, and symbols. The linked TUs were ittaru, itfoxblaster, ityoshieggthrow, itclimbersstring, itclinkmilk, and itkirby_2F23. The PR also made partial matching improvements in itkusudama, itkyasarin, itpikachuthunder, and itwstar. The decomp.dev report showed +5984 matched code bytes, +21216 linked code bytes, +864 matched data bytes, +1120 linked data bytes, and 19 new matches. The PR body noted the remaining item work after this slice: 24 item TUs and 157 item functions.
what_changed: configure.py changed ittaru.c, itfoxblaster.c, ityoshieggthrow.c, itclimbersstring.c, itclinkmilk.c, and itkirby_2F23.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
config/GALE01/symbols.txt was updated to reflect newly linked local functions and local constants, including changing itFoxblaster_UnkMotion8/9/10 Anim/Phys/Coll symbols from global to local scope.
Many formerly global synthetic data symbols were converted to local compiler-style labels such as @149, @284, @323, and ...data.0, especially in .data, .sdata, and .sdata2.

src/melee/it/itCharItems.h refactored the fox blaster item variable layout from repeated scalar fields xDE4/xDE8/... and xDFC/xE00/... plus repeated Vec3 and angle fields into grouped arrays: xDE4[6], xDFC[6], xE14[6], and angle[6].
itfoxblaster.c was rewritten to use the new foxblaster arrays and a simple reverse loop for shifting six history slots instead of unrolled scalar field copies.

itclimbersstring.c removed placeholder and unused includes, replaced extern zero Vec3 constants with local zero initializers, introduced helper inlines for GObj setup and joint attribute access, and reordered/reshaped functions to match.

itclimbersstring.c moved it_802C33B8 and it_802C3520 from static/unmatched-style placement into concrete definitions and reordered it_802C2EC4, cleanup, anim, picked-up, and event functions for matching.
itclinkmilk.c added its ItemStateTable in C, adjusted JObj child/grandchild access, introduced an ip2 temporary in itCLinkMilk_NotifyParent, and used padding changes to satisfy matching.
itkirby_2F23.c added its ItemStateTable in C, moved setup calls out of it_802F23EC_inline into it_802F23EC, and rewrote the scale calculation with temporaries.

ittaru.c added its ItemStateTable in C, included string.h and baselib/random.h directly, introduced sdata_ordering to force U32_TO_F32 placement/order, rewrote a float/reg ordering expression in spawn setup, and passed a zero Vec3 through an inline to avoid a local layout gap.

ityoshieggthrow.c reordered functions, introduced small spawn1/spawn2 inlines around efAsync_Spawn calls, and adjusted local variables/JObj access in it_802B2C38 to close matches for clanked and related functions.
itkusudama.c inlined item spawn helper logic at call sites in it_80289BE8, improving but not fully matching that function.
itkyasarin.c separated HSD_Randi calls into a temporary rand variable and adjusted PAD_STACK size, improving but not fully matching reported functions.
itpikachuthunder.c hoisted locals, rewrote pika_scale with explicit float and integer conversion temporaries, and adjusted padding.
itwstar.c changed candidate-selection locals/order, initialized xDD8 and xDD4 to 1.0f in Logic29_Spawned, and used volatile/local padding changes in it_802947CC.
smart_moves: Grouped sequential foxblaster struct fields into arrays once the access pattern was understood; this enabled idiomatic loop code and removed a previous unrolled-copy workaround.

Used local compiler-style symbol labels and local scope in symbols.txt for constants that became TU-local after linking, avoiding unnecessary global names for floats, doubles, strings, and rodata Vec3 constants.
Moved ItemStateTable definitions into the newly matching item C files where evidence showed the data could now be linked with the TU.
Replaced external zero Vec3 constants in itclimbersstring with local aggregate initializers, which both simplified dependencies and matched symbol locality changes.

Introduced small static inline wrappers for repeated call sequences, such as GObj setup in itclimbersstring and efAsync_Spawn sequences in ityoshieggthrow, to influence generated code without changing behavior.
Used explicit temporary variables, reordered declarations, and PAD_STACK adjustments where the diff shows register allocation or stack layout was the remaining blocker.
Kept partial improvements in nearby unmatched item files in the same PR, allowing several functions/data sections to reach 100% or improve even when their whole TUs were not linked.
decomp_lessons: When six adjacent item-var fields behave as a rolling history, prefer modeling them as arrays in the struct header; this can unlock natural for-loops and eliminate fake unrolled improvements.

For item TUs, matching data often requires correcting symbol scope at the same time as code: local strings/floats/doubles should become @NN labels rather than global it_80xxxxxx names when appropriate.
Do not keep placeholder includes or broad extern constants once the TU can own exact includes and local literals; include hygiene can affect linked-TU confidence and data ownership.
Function order and inline boundaries matter for these item files. Several matches were achieved by moving helper code into or out of static inline functions and by reordering definitions.

Small local-expression rewrites can fix register and sdata ordering issues: examples include separated HSD_Randi temporaries, explicit float-to-int conversion temporaries, volatile locals, and combined assignment ordering.
Data-table linking for item states should be revisited when a TU is near completion; adding ItemStateTable definitions was part of matching ittaru, itclinkmilk, and itkirby_2F23.
PAD_STACK changes remain a legitimate matching tool, but this PR used them alongside semantic layout fixes rather than as the only tactic.
naming_conventions: Preserved existing unknown item function names such as it_802C248C, it_802C33B8, it_80289BE8, it_802B2E7C, and it_802F258C when semantic names were not established.
Used item-specific motion callback names of the form it<ItemName>_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll, e.g. itClinkmilk_UnkMotion1_Anim and itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion2_Anim.
Used logic callback names of the form it_2725_LogicNN_Action for item callbacks, e.g. it_2725_Logic70_PickedUp, it_2725_Logic70_EvtUnk, and it_2725_Logic43_Clanked.

Used descriptive local helper names where evidence was local and narrow, such as it_802C248C_setupGObj, it_802C248C_joint24, it_802C248C_joint28, itClimbersstring_Cleanup, pika_scale, spawn1, and spawn2.
Modeled compiler-local data symbols in symbols.txt with @NN labels and local ...data.0 labels rather than assigning global it_80xxxxxx names to anonymous constants.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed configure.py Matching status only after matching code/data changes were in place for the six item TUs.
Converted repeated foxblaster fields into arrays and used a descending for loop from i = 5 to i > 0 to shift history slots.
Adjusted local variable declaration order and variable lifetimes to influence register allocation, visible in itpikachuthunder, itwstar, itkyasarin, and itclinkmilk.
Used PAD_STACK adjustments in several functions, including itKyasarin_UnkMotion4_Coll_inline/it_802EDDC0, itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion2_Anim, it_802886C4, and climbers string helpers.
Used small static inline wrappers to change call expansion and stack/register behavior without changing observed behavior.
Replaced global rodata zero Vec3 extern references with local Vec3 zero = { 0.0F, 0.0F, 0.0F } initializers where symbols were made local.
Introduced sdata_ordering(u32 u) in ittaru.c with a comment that it ensures U32_TO_F32 is in the right place.
Used volatile u32 in itwstar.c for saved_xDA4, indicating a deliberate ordering/register tactic for matching.
Split arithmetic into explicit temporaries, such as pika_scale's f32 temporary and cast and itkirby_2F23 scale calculation's f and dist reuse.
Adjusted symbol scopes from global to local for newly linked item callbacks and anonymous constants.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body reported progress context only: Remaining item TUs: 24; Remaining item functions: 157.
The decomp.dev bot reported 19 new matches, +0.15% matched code, +0.55% linked code, +0.07% matched data, and +0.09% linked data for GALE01.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that reduce the remaining 24 item TUs after PR 2442.
Search for additional foxblaster struct layout refinements after xDE4[6], xDFC[6], xE14[6], and angle[6].
Look for follow-up cleanup that names it_802C248C, it_802C33B8, it_802C3520, or other climbers string functions.
Search for later PRs that fully link itkusudama, itkyasarin, itpikachuthunder, or itwstar after the partial improvements here.
Find PRs that use sdata_ordering or similar dummy conversion helpers to control U32_TO_F32 or sdata placement.
